summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Knee pain, primarily on the right side, exacerbated by running.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing knee pain primarily in the right knee, particularly when running. He describes the pain as starting during runs and intensifying to the point where he needs to stop. Ice and ibuprofen provide temporary relief. He has been running for years, including 5ks and marathons, and has not previously experienced this level of discomfort. He denies any recent trauma. He reports a family history of immune diseases but is unsure if there is a history of arthritis.

Findings
Blood pressure was 120/60 mmHg. Heart rate was 58 bpm. Respirations were 14 breaths per minute. Pedal pulse was strong and rhythmic. Physical examination revealed no redness or swelling in the right knee. A small amount of effusion was appreciated. Knee movement elicited pain with pushing and pulling, but no pain with passive range of motion. The right knee caused pain with external pressure. No numbness or tingling was noted in the feet.

Assessment
The patient has a knee sprain from overuse, likely exacerbated by recent running activity. There is no evidence of fracture or arthritis.

Plan
I am ordering physical therapy for your right knee to strengthen the lower extremities and recommend good running exercises. I would like you to stay on two tylenol five hundred milligrams and two ibuprofen two hundred milligram tablets, taking them three times a day. I would like to schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess your progress. Please refrain from running for two weeks and consult with the physical therapist for appropriate exercises. Do you have any questions?
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Right knee pain.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Patient reports she is an avid runner who enjoys participating in marathons.

FAMILY HISTORY

Patient denies any known family history of arthritis or other immune disease.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Denies fever.
Musculoskeletal: Reports right knee pain.

VITALS

Blood pressure: 120/60 mmHg
Heart rate: 58 bpm
Respiration: 14
No fever.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Respiratory
- Assessment of Respiratory Effort: Normal respiratory effort.

Cardiovascular
- Examination: Normal pedal pulses in right knee.
- Auscultation of Heart: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, gallops or rubs.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema.
- Right knee: No pain with movement. No pain with strength testing. No erythema or edema. Small effusion. Tender to palpation over the lateral aspect of the knee.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right knee were obtained and reviewed in office today. These reveal well-maintained joint spaces. There is no evidence of any fracture or arthritis.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Right knee sprain.
- Medical Reasoning: The patient is an avid runner and her symptoms appear to be the result of overuse.
- Patient Education and Counseling: We discussed the nature of her diagnosis, as well as her x-ray results, in detail. I advised her that this is a common issue for runners and encouraged her to continue her strength training exercises.
- Medical Treatment: She will take Tylenol 500 mg, 2 tablets, and ibuprofen 200 mg, 2 tablets, 3 times daily to reduce her pain and inflammation. We will also refer her to physical therapy to help strengthen her lower extremities, work on her balance, and demonstrate proper running exercises. She should avoid running for the next 2 weeks until her follow up visit, at which point we can consider adding some additional activity.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

INSTRUCTIONS

The patient will follow up in 2 weeks.
